meta.title: Privacy Statement — Vincor
meta.description: Vincor's privacy statement. How we handle your personal data and health data under the GDPR.

hero.title: Privacy Statement
hero.description: How Vincor handles your personal data, in particular health data, in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

sections.title: 1. Who is the controller?
sections.content: Vincor (Vinculum Corporis) is a clinic for occlusion and posture diagnostics based in Eindhoven. We are the data controller under the GDPR for the data we process about you.
list: Address: Dokter Cuyperslaan 76, 5623 BB Eindhoven, The Netherlands
Chamber of Commerce (KvK) number: [KvK number to be completed]
Privacy contact: Dirk [surname], owner
Email for privacy enquiries: info@vincorscan.nl

sections.title: 2. Which data do we process?
sections.content:
We distinguish between general personal data and special-category data (health data under GDPR article 9).

General personal data (collected through the website):
list: Name, email address and phone number
Subject and content of messages sent through the contact form
IP address (via Cloudflare, for security)
Cookie preferences (stored locally in your browser)
sections.additionalContent: Special-category data — health data (article 9 GDPR): complaints you describe in the contact form, plus diagnostic records, treatment plans, correspondence with other clinicians, Shining 3D scans, photos and reports — the latter held only in the patient file at the practice.

sections.title: 3. Why do we process these data?
sections.content: We use your data only for clearly defined purposes, each based on a specific legal basis under the GDPR:
list: Replying to general contact-form enquiries — article 6(1)(f) (legitimate interest)
Contact form with health context — article 9(2)(a) (your explicit consent via the checkbox)
Treatment agreement and patient file — articles 6(1)(b) and 9(2)(h) (healthcare provision)
Invoicing and bookkeeping — article 6(1)(c) (legal obligation, tax retention)
Website security — article 6(1)(f) (legitimate interest)
Analytics cookies — article 6(1)(a) (your consent via cookie banner)

sections.title: 4. How long do we keep your data?
sections.content: We keep your data no longer than necessary. Retention per category:
list: Contact-form lead that does not become a patient — 6 months
Patient file — 20 years after last treatment (Dutch WGBO, Civil Code 7:454)
Financial records — 7 years (Dutch Tax Act article 52)
Cloudflare logs including IP — 30 days
Cookie preferences — 12 months or until you clear them

sections.title: 5. Who do we share your data with?
sections.content: We share your data only with processors we rely on to deliver our services. A data-processing agreement under article 28 GDPR is in place with each of them.
list: Resend (United States, EU region where available) — sending emails from the contact form
Cloudflare (United States, European edge servers) — hosting, Workers, DNS, security and optional analytics
GitHub (United States) — OAuth authentication for content editing via Decap CMS
Google (United States) — Google Maps embed, loaded only after your consent
PDA (EU) — only for phase 3 (reconstruction), with your consent
Balans Rugcentrum (EU) — for performing 4D posture scans
sections.additionalContent: Patient-file data is not shared with hosting or contact-form providers. Sharing with other clinicians occurs only with your consent or on the legal basis of article 9(2)(h) GDPR (healthcare provision).

sections.title: 6. Transfers outside the European Union
sections.content: Resend, Cloudflare, GitHub and Google may process data in the United States. We rely on: (a) the EU-US Data Privacy Framework where the party is certified, or (b) Standard Contractual Clauses under Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2021/914. Partners inside the EU — including Dr. Arash Zarrinpour in Paris — are covered by the GDPR directly and require no additional safeguards.

sections.title: 7. Your rights
sections.content: The GDPR gives you the following specific rights regarding your personal data:
list: Right of access (article 15) — ask which data we hold about you
Right to rectification (article 16) — correct inaccurate data
Right to erasure (article 17) — delete your data, subject to our statutory retention obligations
Right to restriction of processing (article 18)
Right to object (article 21)
Right to data portability (article 20)
Right to withdraw consent (article 7(3)) — without affecting the lawfulness of previous processing
sections.additionalContent: To exercise any of these rights, email info@vincorscan.nl. We respond within 30 days (article 12(3) GDPR). To prevent misuse we may ask for additional identification before sharing information.

sections.title: 8. Lodging a complaint
sections.content: If you are not satisfied with how we handle your data, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) via autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl, PO Box 93374, 2509 AJ The Hague. Patients from France may also contact CNIL at cnil.fr.

sections.title: 9. Cookies
sections.content: We only set cookies that are required for the website to function properly. For statistics and Google Maps we ask your consent in advance via the cookie banner.
list: Essential (session, security, cookie preferences themselves) — no consent required
Statistics (Cloudflare Web Analytics) — only after your opt-in
Google Maps (map on the contact page) — loaded only after your consent
sections.additionalContent: You manage your preferences through the cookie banner or via the "Cookie preferences" link at the bottom of the page. Withdrawing consent is as easy as giving it.

sections.title: 10. Data breaches
sections.content: In the event of a data breach we notify the Dutch Data Protection Authority within 72 hours (article 33 GDPR). If the breach poses a high risk to your rights and freedoms we will also inform you personally (article 34 GDPR).

sections.title: 11. Security measures
sections.content: We take appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your data (article 32 GDPR):
list: HTTPS (TLS 1.3) across the whole website
Access control on patient files — clinicians only
Two-factor authentication on administrator accounts
Regular backups on encrypted storage
Data-processing agreements with all suppliers
Annual review by the owner

sections.title: 12. No Data Protection Officer (DPO)
sections.content: Vincor is a small practice and does not process health data on a large scale within the meaning of article 37(1)(c) GDPR. EDPB guideline WP243 explicitly states that an individual clinician in a small practice does not qualify as large-scale processing. A DPO is therefore not required. Our internal memo is available on request via info@vincorscan.nl.

sections.title: 13. Changes
sections.content: We may update this privacy statement as our practice or legislation evolves. The current version is always available on the privacy page of our website. We will proactively inform you about material changes.

sections.title: 14. Privacy contact
sections.content: Any questions about this privacy statement or about the processing of your personal data? Please contact us:
